steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The prompt asks to emphasize the order in which the supercontinents formed — specifically which came first and which came later.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Vaalbara and Euramerica were both supercontinents, single landmasses made up of most or all of Earth's continents.: lists both supercontinents but provides no temporal link between them. ✗
  • Choice B — The supercontinent Euramerica formed long after the supercontinent Vaalbara.: directly states that Euramerica came much later than Vaalbara, capturing exactly the temporal order the prompt requires. ✓
  • Choice C — Forming and breaking apart over hundreds of millions of years, supercontinents are made up of most or all of Earth's continents.: describes the general supercontinent cycle rather than the specific order of these two. ✗
  • Choice D — Vaalbara formed about 3.6 billion years ago but eventually broke apart.: mentions only one supercontinent, so no order can be shown. ✗
B is correct.
